10. KEY WEAKNESSES & RISKS (Detailed)
Top Weakness #1
NPL EXPOSURE — Some non-performing loan risk in public sector portfolio; sovereign/parastatal concentration
UEMOA CONCENTRATION — Heavy reliance on UEMOA zone; limited diversification outside West Africa
NEW ENTITY RISK — Rebranded 2023; limited track record as AFG Bank CI; compliance maturity still developing
PARENT EXIT RISK — SG has sold Cameroon (2023) and Ghana (2023) subsidiaries; CI exit risk real despite top market position; must be monitored
NO LOCAL PRESENCE FOR COLLECTIONS — Single Abidjan office; cannot support rural or even semi-urban collections; must partner with SIB/SG for all CI collections
LIMITED CI FOOTPRINT — CIB-only; 1 Abidjan office; no retail banking; limited local XOF liquidity
SMALLER BALANCE SHEET — #7 in UEMOA by total assets; less capacity for very large structured transactions vs. top-3
NOT A LICENSED BANK — No BCEAO deposit protection; no guaranteed fund safety; regulatory risk if MTN restructures MoMo
Top Weakness #2
DIGITAL MATURITY — SIBNET functional but behind CitiDirect/SG in sophistication; limited ERP connectors
LESS DIFFERENTIATED FX — No specialist FX hedging capability; basic forwards only
COMPLIANCE TRACK RECORD — AML/CFT maturity still developing as new entity
YUP DISCONTINUED — Rural collection strategy weakened after YUP mobile money shut down March 2022; current rural approach is standard branches only
HIGH MINIMUM THRESHOLDS — Citi in CI typically requires large FX/payment volumes to justify relationship; not suited for smaller corporates
NO RETAIL BANKING — Cannot serve consumer segments; limited for companies needing payroll distribution to unbanked workers
MODERATE DIGITAL MATURITY — Business Online less sophisticated than CitiDirect or SG platform
AML / KYC STANDARDS — Different KYC standards vs licensed banks; compliance risk for corporate clients using MoMo for bulk collections
Top Weakness #3
PUBLIC SECTOR INFLUENCE — Ivorian state as co-shareholder (25%) creates potential conflicts; political lending risk
INVESTMENT BANKING — Weaker structured finance capability vs. SG/Citi for complex transactions
SMALL BALANCE SHEET — Limited capacity for large credit facilities or complex structured products
TRADE FEES — ~1–2% trade finance fees; not the cheapest for very high-volume commodity traders
EXPENSIVE FOR SMALL VOLUMES — Premium pricing suitable for large flows; SME or medium companies may find Citi too expensive
RECENT ENTRANT — Only in CI since 2017; shorter local relationship track record vs. SG (60+ years) or SIB (60+ years)
BMCE INTEGRATION — BMCE/BOA ongoing integration creates some operational uncertainty; some legacy systems
FUND SAFETY — Mobile wallet funds not BCEAO-insured like bank deposits; corporate risk exposure
Top Weakness #4 (Specific to FX / International Payments)
FX HEDGING LIMITED — No options or structured FX products; limited forward tenor beyond 12 months
FX HEDGING LIMITED — Basic forwards only; not suited for complex hedging programmes
FX VERY LIMITED — Not recommended for large FX management
FX GOOD BUT NOT CITI — SG FX competitive but not the same depth/pricing as Citi for very large USD/EUR flows
XOF LIQUIDITY — Single CI office means Citi may have less XOF day-to-day liquidity vs. SG or SIB with large local deposit bases
FX HEDGING IN CI — Standard Bank's CI FX capability less proven than SG or Citi; relatively new in market
FX VERY LIMITED — Not suitable as primary FX bank for large international payments
NO FX — Cannot handle any corporate FX
11. OVERALL SUITABILITY SUMMARY
Best Suited For
Large local/regional corps; UEMOA agri-trade; nationwide CI collection banking (branch-based); cocoa/coffee campaign financing; UEMOA regional payments
Mid-size corporates; UEMOA regional payments; digital-first companies; SMEs; local investment banking via Atlantique Finance
SMEs + institutions; digital-first CI companies; local sovereign/political relationships; companies valuing Ivorian-owned bank
Multinationals; full-service corporate banking in CI; structured finance; custody / sub-custody (SGSS CI Best 2025); local + international bonds
Multinationals; sovereign-linked entities; trade-intensive (cocoa/agri exporters); Eurobond / international capital markets; companies needing world-class FX + SWIFT GPI + MT940/MT942
Multinationals needing Africa-wide CIB; structured + project finance; companies with 5+ African country needs; ISO 20022 / Finacle users
Mid-large corporates; commodity trade; pan-African BMCE operations; companies with BOA UK international commodity trade links
Consumer/SME mobile payments; payroll to unbanked; last-mile rural collections; financial inclusion; agricultural payment corridors
Not Suited For
Companies needing world-class FX hedging or Eurobond access; companies needing CitiDirect-level treasury portals
Companies needing deep rural CI collections or sophisticated FX hedging
Large FX / international payments; complex structured finance; rural collections

⚠️ Any company needing rural or semi-urban collections in CI; SMEs below Citi's minimum threshold
⚠️ Retail banking; rural collections; consumer payments; companies needing broad CI branch coverage
Companies needing best-in-class FX, treasury portals, or rural CI collections
⚠️ Any company needing a licensed bank; large FX; bonds; regulated deposits; NOT suitable as sole banking provider
